DE.admissionRequirements: Higher education entrance qualification (Abitur or recognised foreign equivalent). Most programmes require German B2–C1 OR English proof (IELTS ~6.0–6.5) for English-taught courses. Some restrict NC (numerus clausus) programmes. Apply via uni-assist or directly.
DE.visaWorkRights: Non-EU: student visa + blocked account (~€11,904/year, 2024 figure — verify current amount). EU/EEA: no visa. Work: up to 140 full or 280 half days/year; mini-jobs allowed with limits.
DE.housingNotes: Student dorms (Studierendenwerk): ~€250–400/month — apply early, long waiting lists in big cities. Private shared flat (WG): ~€400–700 in Berlin/Munich. DAAD accommodation guide linked below.

deadlines.note: Many unis; some earlier for NC subjects
Not all programmes offer summer intake

NO.admissionRequirements: Upper secondary equivalent to Norwegian vitnemål. English: IELTS 6.0+ or TOEFL 80+ typical for English programmes. Each university sets own requirements — check programme page.
NO.visaWorkRights: Non-EU: study permit via UDI. EU/EEA: register after 3 months. Work: up to 20 hours/week during term with permit; full-time in holidays.
NO.housingNotes: Student housing (SiO etc.): ~NOK 3,500–6,000/month. High demand in Oslo — apply months ahead. Private rent expensive in Oslo/Bergen.

FI.admissionRequirements: Secondary diploma recognised in Finland. English programmes: IELTS 6.0–6.5 typical. Apply via Studyinfo.fi for most bachelor's; direct to uni for many master's.
FI.visaWorkRights: Non-EU: residence permit for studies. EU: register locally. Work: up to 30 hours/week on average during term with valid permit.
FI.housingNotes: Student housing (HOAS, TYS, etc.): ~€300–550/month in Helsinki. Apply immediately after admission — queues are long.

SE.admissionRequirements: Completed upper secondary. English: IELTS 6.5 or equivalent for English programmes. Apply via universityadmissions.se for bachelor's; direct for many master's.
SE.visaWorkRights: Non-EU: residence permit. EU: right of residence if studying. Work: no formal hour cap for students with residence permit, but studies must remain priority.
SE.housingNotes: Student corridor rooms: ~SEK 3,500–6,500/month. Stockholm queue can be 1–2 years — join queue early via SSCO member housing.

NL.admissionRequirements: Diploma equivalent to Dutch VWO/HBO entry. English: IELTS 6.0–6.5 typical. Numerus fixus programmes have earlier deadlines and may use lottery or selection.
NL.visaWorkRights: Non-EU: entry visa + residence permit (uni often sponsors). EU: no visa. Work: up to 16 hours/week during term; full-time in June–August without extra permit.
NL.housingNotes: Huge shortage nationally. Student rooms: €400–800/month. Start searching before arrival; university often has waiting lists only.

PL.admissionRequirements: Maturity certificate / secondary diploma. English programmes: IELTS 5.5–6.5 typical. Some require entrance exams or interviews.
PL.visaWorkRights: Non-EU: national visa D + temporary residence for studies. EU: free movement. Work: possible with residence card; check permit conditions.
PL.housingNotes: Dorms: ~€80–150/month (very cheap). Private rent in Warsaw/Kraków: €300–500. Dorms fill fast — apply with admission.

CZ.admissionRequirements: Secondary school leaving certificate. Czech programmes: B2 Czech often required. English programmes: IELTS 6.0 typical. Some faculties require entrance exams.
CZ.visaWorkRights: Non-EU: long-term visa + residence for studies. EU: notify police within 30 days. Work: notify immigration when taking employment.
CZ.housingNotes: Dorms: ~€150–250/month. Private in Prague: €400–700. University dorm application usually with admission.

AT.admissionRequirements: University entrance qualification (Matura or equivalent). German C1 often required for German programmes; English programmes need IELTS ~6.0–6.5.
AT.visaWorkRights: Non-EU: residence permit for students. EU: free movement. Work: limited hours with permit — verify current limits with OeAD.
AT.housingNotes: OeAD housing service helps international students. Dorms: ~€300–500/month in Vienna. Start early.

IT.admissionRequirements: Secondary diploma (12 years). English master's: IELTS 6.0–6.5. May need pre-enrollment on Universitaly.it for visa. Some programmes require entrance test.
IT.visaWorkRights: Non-EU: type D visa + permesso di soggiorno. EU: register locally. Work: part-time allowed with restrictions on study permit.
IT.housingNotes: DSU regional housing: €200–400/month subsidised. Private in Milan/Rome: €500–900. Apply to DSU after enrollment.

ES.admissionRequirements: Bachillerato or equivalent (homologación may be needed). English programmes: IELTS 6.0–6.5. Selectividad or UNEDasiss for some international applicants.
ES.visaWorkRights: Non-EU: student visa (stays over 90 days). EU: register. Work: up to 30 hours/week with authorization after first year in many cases.
ES.housingNotes: University residences: €350–650/month. Shared flat in Madrid/Barcelona: €400–700. Residencias have limited spots.

PT.admissionRequirements: Secondary education certificate. English programmes: IELTS 6.0 typical. National access exam may apply to some bachelor's.
PT.visaWorkRights: Non-EU: residence visa for study. EU: free movement. Work: possible with residence permit conditions.
PT.housingNotes: University residences: €180–350/month. Lisbon/Porto private: €400–700. SASUL/residence applications via university.

FR.admissionRequirements: Baccalauréat or equivalent. English programmes: IELTS 6.0–6.5. Bachelor via Parcoursup (French system); international master's often direct. Campus France process for many non-EU applicants.
FR.visaWorkRights: Non-EU: VLS-TS étudiant via Campus France. EU: no visa. Work: 964 hours/year (~60% full-time) on student visa.
FR.housingNotes: CROUS student housing: ~€150–400/month — apply early via DSE. Private in Paris: €600–900+. CAF housing aid available.

IE.admissionRequirements: Leaving Certificate or equivalent. English: IELTS 6.0–6.5 typical (often 6.5 for competitive programmes). CAO for undergraduate.
IE.visaWorkRights: Non-EU: IRP (stamp 2) after registration. Work: 20 hours/week during term, 40 hours holidays.
IE.housingNotes: Campus accommodation: €600–900/month in Dublin. Severe shortage — start search months before. On-campus not guaranteed.

GB.admissionRequirements: A-levels/IB or equivalent. English: IELTS 6.0–7.0 depending on university. UCAS for most undergraduate (Jan deadline for equal consideration).
GB.visaWorkRights: Student visa required for most non-UK nationals. Work: 20 hours/week during term for degree-level students on student visa.
GB.housingNotes: Halls: £100–250/week. London very expensive. Guaranteed halls often for first-years only — apply when you accept offer.

DK.admissionRequirements: Upper secondary equivalent. English: IELTS 6.5 typical. Apply via optagelse.dk for bachelor's (quota 1/2 system).
DK.visaWorkRights: Non-EU: residence permit. EU: free movement. Work: 20 hours/week Sep–May; full-time June–August.
DK.housingNotes: Akademikerbolig/college rooms: DKK 2,500–4,500/month. Copenhagen extremely competitive — join waiting lists early.

BE.admissionRequirements: Secondary diploma. Language: Dutch/French/English depending on community and programme. IELTS 6.0–6.5 for English programmes.
BE.visaWorkRights: Non-EU: student visa via university invitation. EU: free movement. Work: allowed with restrictions — check commune rules.
BE.housingNotes: Kot/student room: €350–600/month. Brussels/Leuven competitive. University housing offices help.

HU.admissionRequirements: Secondary school certificate. English programmes: IELTS 5.5–6.0 typical. Some require entrance exam or Skype interview.
HU.visaWorkRights: Non-EU: residence permit for study. EU: free movement. Work: possible with permit — notify immigration.
HU.housingNotes: Dorms: €100–200/month (affordable). Budapest private: €300–500. Apply for dorm with admission letter.

EE.admissionRequirements: Secondary education certificate. English: IELTS 5.5–6.5. Apply via DreamApply or directly to university.
EE.visaWorkRights: Non-EU: D-visa + temporary residence. EU: free movement. Work: allowed with residence permit conditions.
EE.housingNotes: Dorms: €80–150/month. Tallinn private: €300–500. University dorm application after admission.

LT.admissionRequirements: Brandos atestatas or equivalent. English programmes: IELTS 6.0 typical. Apply via LAMA BPO or directly.
LT.visaWorkRights: Non-EU: national visa D + temporary residence. EU: free movement. Work: check migration conditions.
LT.housingNotes: Dorms: €70–150/month. Vilnius private: €250–450. Cheap compared to Western Europe.

SI.admissionRequirements: Matura or equivalent. Slovenian for most bachelor's; English for selected master's. IELTS 6.0 for English programmes.
SI.visaWorkRights: Non-EU: temporary residence for study. EU: free movement. Work: notify authorities when employed.
SI.housingNotes: Student dorms: €100–200/month. Ljubljana private: €350–550. Limited dorm capacity.

CH.admissionRequirements: Recognised secondary certificate. Language: German/French/English by programme. IELTS 6.5+ for English programmes. Some require entrance exam.
CH.visaWorkRights: Non-EU: national D visa then residence permit. Work: 15 hours/week during term with employer notification; full-time holidays.
CH.housingNotes: Very expensive. Student housing: CHF 500–900/month. Start search immediately after admission.

RO.admissionRequirements: Baccalaureate or equivalent. English programmes: IELTS 6.0 typical. May require admission exam or file evaluation.
RO.visaWorkRights: Non-EU: long-stay visa + residence. EU: free movement. Work: part-time possible with permit.
RO.housingNotes: Dorms: €50–150/month. Bucharest private: €250–450. Very affordable housing.

BG.admissionRequirements: Diploma za sredno obrazovanie or equivalent. English programmes: IELTS 6.0 typical. Certificate recognition may be required.
BG.visaWorkRights: Non-EU: type D visa + residence for education. EU: free movement. Work: check permit conditions.
BG.housingNotes: Dorms: €30–80/month. Sofia private: €200–400. Among cheapest in EU.

HR.admissionRequirements: Matura or equivalent. Croatian for most programmes; English for selected. IELTS 6.0 for English programmes.
HR.visaWorkRights: Non-EU: temporary stay for study. EU: free movement. Work: notify police when working.
HR.housingNotes: Dorms: €80–150/month. Zagreb private: €300–500. Apply via university housing office.

SK.admissionRequirements: Maturita or equivalent. Slovak for most bachelor's; English for selected programmes. IELTS 6.0 typical.
SK.visaWorkRights: Non-EU: temporary residence for study. EU: free movement. Work: permitted with conditions on residence.
SK.housingNotes: Dorms: €80–150/month. Bratislava private: €400–600. Affordable compared to Western EU.

LV.admissionRequirements: Atestāts or equivalent. English programmes: IELTS 6.0 typical. Apply via Latvian university portals.
LV.visaWorkRights: Non-EU: long-term visa + residence permit. EU: free movement. Work: check OCMA rules.

GR.admissionRequirements: Apolytirio or equivalent. Most programmes in Greek; English for selected master's. IELTS 6.0–6.5 for English programmes.
GR.visaWorkRights: Non-EU: national visa + residence for studies. EU: free movement. Work: part-time allowed with restrictions.
GR.housingNotes: Dorms limited and cheap where available. Athens private: €350–600. ASOEE/HEI housing varies.

IS.admissionRequirements: Stúdentspróf or equivalent. English widely used; IELTS 6.0–6.5 for proof when required.
IS.visaWorkRights: Non-EEA: residence permit for studies. EEA: free movement. Work: permitted with residence permit.
IS.housingNotes: Expensive. Student housing via Félagsstofnun studenta or university: ISK 80,000–120,000/month. High cost of living.

LU.admissionRequirements: Diplôme de fin d'études or equivalent. English/French/German by programme. IELTS 6.5 for English programmes.
LU.visaWorkRights: Non-EU: temporary authorisation to stay for studies. EU: free movement. Work: 40 hours/week during holidays; 15 hours/week during term for non-EU.
LU.housingNotes: Very expensive. University of Luxembourg assists with CROUS-style listings. Budget €800–1,200/month housing.

MT.admissionRequirements: Matriculation certificate or equivalent. English programmes: IELTS 6.0 typical. UM and MCAST have different processes.
MT.visaWorkRights: Non-EU: student visa/residence. EU: free movement. Work: 20 hours/week during term possible with permit.
MT.housingNotes: University residence available but limited. Private: €400–700/month. Apply early for campus housing.

CY.admissionRequirements: Apolytirion or equivalent. English widely used. IELTS 6.0–6.5 typical for formal proof.
CY.visaWorkRights: Non-EU: student visa via migration department. EU: free movement. Work: restrictions apply on student permit.
CY.housingNotes: University halls available at UCY, CUT, etc. Private: €400–700/month in Nicosia/Limassol.
